subject: Science
question_text: Paheli wants to present her friend a gift made of plant-fibre. Which out of the following will she select?
answer_explanation: Jute is a plant fibre. Wool and silk are animal fibres, and nylon is a synthetic fibre.

subject: Science
question_text: Which statement out of the following is incorrect?
options: Use of Charkha was popularised by Mahatma Gandhi as a part of the Independence Movement.
In India, jute is mainly grown in Kerala and Punjab.
To make fabric, the fibres are first converted into yarns.
Sufi saint Kabir was a weaver.
answer_key: In India, jute is mainly grown in Kerala and Punjab.
answer_explanation: Jute requires a lot of water and is mainly grown in West Bengal, Bihar, and Assam, not Kerala and Punjab.
provenance_excerpt: Which statement out of the following is incorrect?

subject: Science
question_text: Once, Paheli visited a tailor shop and brought home some cuttings of fabric to study their properties. She took two pieces and found that one of the pieces were shrinking when it was burnt with a candle. However the other did not shrink on burning. Can you help her to find out which of the two was a cotton fabric and which a silk fabric?
answer_key: The piece that shrank on burning is the silk fabric (it burns like hair and shrinks). The piece that did not shrink is the cotton fabric (it burns like paper).
answer_explanation: Burn test properties of fibres.

question_text: Boojho with perfect eyesight was finding it difficult to pass a thread through the eye of a needle. What can be the possible reason for this?

answer_explanation: Practical application of fibre/yarn structure.

The yarn was ________ to give beautiful colours and then ________ to get cotton fabric.

subject: Science
question_text:
Match the terms given in Column I with the statements given in Column II.
(a) Weaving (b) Knitting (c) Spinning (d) Ginning (e) Fibre (f) Yarn
---
(i) A single yarn used to make a fabric (ii) Combing of cotton fibres to remove seeds (iii) Yarns are made from these thin strands (iv) These are spun from fibres and then used to make fabrics (v) Process of arranging two sets of yarns together to make a fabric (vi) Process of making yarn from fibres
answer_key:
(a) Weaving - (v)
(b) Knitting - (i)
(c) Spinning - (vi)
(d) Ginning - (ii)
(e) Fibre - (iii)
(f) Yarn - (iv)
answer_explanation: Definitions of textile processes.

subject: Science
question_text: A cotton shirt, before it reaches you, completes a long journey. Elaborate this journey starting from cotton bolls.
answer_key:
1. Cotton bolls are picked from plants.
2. Ginning: Fibres are separated from seeds.
3. Spinning: Fibres are spun to create yarn.
4. Weaving/Knitting: Yarn is woven on looms to make cotton fabric.
5. Dyeing/Printing: Fabric is dyed with colours.
6. Stitching: Fabric is cut and stitched into a shirt.
answer_explanation: Comprehensive understanding of textile manufacturing process.
provenance_excerpt: A cotton shirt, before it reaches you, completes a long journey. Elaborate this journey starting from cotton bolls.

subject: Science
question_text: Describe the two main processes of making fabric from yarn.
answer_key:
1. Weaving: The process of arranging two sets of yarns together (perpendicular to each other) to make a fabric. It is done on looms.
2. Knitting: A single yarn is used to make a piece of fabric. It can be done by hand using knitting needles or on machines.
answer_explanation: Difference between weaving and knitting.
provenance_excerpt: Describe the two main processes of making fabric from yarn.
